mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term shows negative predictive accuracy over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days exclusively during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative or flat forward returns during early-cycle periods, or predictive accuracy drops below baseline when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in over 60% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when inflation breaches the 2.5% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive edge during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns turn negative when both series expand simultaneously over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansion regimes.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns during late-cycle periods, or predictive accuracy vanishes when applied outside late-cycle regimes.
